title: Diagonal Supersymmetry for Coinvariant Rings
why_relevant: Proves the diagonal supersymmetry theorem: bosonic-fermionic coinvariant rings have universal super-Schur coefficients independent of the numbers of bosonic and fermionic variable sets. Taking one bosonic and one fermionic set isolates exactly hook-shaped Schur coefficients, making this a direct bridge from the original coefficients c_lambda(n) to superspace coinvariants.

title: The Hilbert series of the superspace coinvariant ring
why_relevant: Computes the doubly graded Hilbert series of the one-boson/one-fermion superspace coinvariant ring and proves an operator theorem for its harmonics. Via diagonal supersymmetry, this is one of the most direct sources for extracting and interpreting hook coefficients c_(a,1^b)(n).

title: Superspace coinvariants and hyperplane arrangements
why_relevant: Gives the first explicit monomial basis for the superspace coinvariant ring, proving the Sagan-Swanson basis conjecture. This basis turns the Hilbert-series data needed for hook coefficients into an explicit combinatorial counting problem.

title: A proof of the Fields Conjectures
why_relevant: Determines the bigraded symmetric-group structure of the superspace coinvariant ring and identifies its ungraded structure with a sign-twisted action on ordered set partitions. This supplies refined combinatorial models that can be specialized to the Hilbert-series hook coefficients.

title: The Bosonic-Fermionic Diagonal Coinvariant Modules Conjecture
why_relevant: Formulates the bosonic-fermionic framework and the conjectural principle that hook and more general super-Schur coefficients can be recovered from mixed bosonic/fermionic diagonal coinvariants. It is the conceptual predecessor to the supersymmetry result now used to reduce hook coefficients to superspace.

title: Bosonic-Fermionic Diagonal Coinvariants and Theta Operators
why_relevant: Develops conjectural and computational links between bosonic-fermionic diagonal coinvariant modules and Theta/Macdonald operators. It contains many of the operator identities and special cases relevant to interpreting mixed bosonic-fermionic Hilbert and Frobenius series.

title: A proof of the fermionic Theta coinvariant conjecture
why_relevant: Proves a fermionic case of the Theta-operator coinvariant conjectures, giving techniques for handling anticommuting variables in diagonal coinvariant quotients. These methods are relevant to proofs that pass from ordinary Schur coefficients to hook coefficients through fermionic variables.

title: The super nabla operator
why_relevant: Introduces a unified Macdonald-eigenoperator framework containing nabla, Delta, and Theta operators as specializations. Since superspace and bosonic-fermionic coinvariant formulas are expressed through these operators, this is useful for translating hook-coefficient identities into symmetric-function identities.

title: Multivariate Diagonal Coinvariant Spaces for Complex Reflection Groups
why_relevant: Establishes the universal Schur expansion viewpoint for multivariate diagonal coinvariant Hilbert series and explains why coefficients are independent of the number of variable sets. This is foundational for the coefficients c_lambda(n) appearing in the problem.

title: (GL_k x S_n)-Modules of Multivariate Diagonal Harmonics
why_relevant: Studies diagonal harmonics as simultaneous GL_k and S_n modules and formulates structural decompositions relevant to universal Schur coefficients. It is especially useful for understanding how GL_k characters encode multigraded Hilbert series.

title: (GL_k x S_n)-modules and Nabla of hook-indexed Schur functions
why_relevant: Focuses specifically on GL_k x S_n structures and nabla applied to hook-indexed Schur functions. Its hook-specific multiplicity formulas and conjectures are directly aligned with the hook-shape case of c_lambda(n).

title: Conjectures on the Quotient Ring by Diagonal Invariants
why_relevant: Introduces the diagonal coinvariant ring for the symmetric group and formulates the basic Hilbert-series and representation-theoretic conjectures. It is the foundational source for the algebraic object R_n and its diagonal-invariant quotient construction.

title: Hilbert schemes, polygraphs, and the Macdonald positivity conjecture
why_relevant: Proves the n! conjecture and Macdonald positivity using Hilbert schemes and isospectral Hilbert schemes. These geometric methods underpin the positivity and representation-theoretic interpretation of diagonal coinvariant modules.

title: Vanishing theorems and character formulas for the Hilbert scheme of points in the plane
why_relevant: Proves the character formula for diagonal harmonics and, in particular, the dimension formula for the two-variable diagonal coinvariant ring. This is the main geometric theorem connecting diagonal coinvariants to nabla/Macdonald theory.

title: A Combinatorial Formula for the Character of the Diagonal Coinvariants
why_relevant: Gives the shuffle-conjecture combinatorial formula for the bigraded Frobenius characteristic of diagonal coinvariants and proves key Schur-positivity properties. Its parking-function methods are part of the standard combinatorial toolbox for coinvariant Hilbert and Frobenius series.

title: A proof of the shuffle conjecture
why_relevant: Proves the compositional shuffle conjecture, thereby validating the main parking-function formula for the diagonal coinvariant algebra. This supplies proven combinatorial control over the nabla side that underlies later Delta, Theta, and superspace formulas.

title: The Delta Conjecture
why_relevant: Introduces the Delta conjecture, a major generalization of the shuffle theorem whose specializations govern ordered set partitions and superspace coinvariant Frobenius series. The one-boson/one-fermion superspace formulas used for hook coefficients are closely tied to Delta-operator specializations.

title: Ordered set partitions, generalized coinvariant algebras, and the Delta Conjecture
why_relevant: Constructs generalized coinvariant algebras whose combinatorics are controlled by ordered set partitions and identifies their Frobenius series with a t=0 Delta specialization. Ordered set partitions are a natural combinatorial model for the superspace Hilbert and Frobenius series relevant to hook coefficients.

title: A proof of the Delta Conjecture when q=0
why_relevant: Proves a key specialization of the Delta conjecture and gives combinatorial formulas for the relevant Delta-operator expressions. These t=0/q=0 Delta specializations appear in formulas for superspace coinvariants and hence in hook-coefficient extraction.

title: A module for the Delta conjecture
why_relevant: Defines a super-diagonal coinvariant module conjecturally realizing Delta-conjecture symmetric functions. It is an important precursor to later bosonic-fermionic coinvariant modules and provides representation-theoretic intuition for fermionic refinements.

title: Schedules and the Delta Conjecture
why_relevant: Develops schedule formulas for Delta-conjecture Hilbert series and proposes bases for super-diagonal coinvariant rings. These schedule and basis techniques are useful for turning operator expressions into explicit combinatorial counts.

title: A polynomial expression for the Hilbert series of the quotient ring of diagonal coinvariants
why_relevant: Transforms Haiman's rational-function Hilbert-series formula for diagonal coinvariants into an explicit polynomial and gives constant-term formulations. The paper provides useful algebraic and symmetric-function techniques for extracting Hilbert-series coefficients.
